item.description: Hi 👋 I am Ana Ezquerro. I am a Master Student at University of A Coruña and a part-time researcher in the LyS Group. Recently graduated in Data Science and Engineering (2019-2023) and currently enrolled in the first year of the Master in Artificial Intelligence. Planning a PhD degree on Human-like Language Representations. Previous work: Natural Language Processing and Multimodal Learning. Python as main programming language with proficiency level in Julia, R & SQL and advanced level in Java.

🪐 Honors 2019. Best Academic Records for the University Test Access in Galicia offered by the University of Santiago de Compostela. 2022. Department Collaboration Scholarship offered by the University of A Coruña and Banco Santander. 2022. Awards for Academic Excellence in Bachelor’s Degrees 2020-21 offered by Xunta de Galicia. 2023. Awards for Academic Excellence in Bachelor’s Degrees 2021-22 offered by Xunta de Galicia.

🚀 Research Ana Ezquerro, Germán Aneiros-Pérez, Manuel Oviedo. Automatic covariates selection in dynamic regression models with application to COVID-19 evolution. In XoveTIC V Conference founded by the Centre for Information and Communications Technology Research (CITIC) with the University of A Coruña (UDC). Ana Ezquerro, Carlos Gómez-Rodríguez, and David Vilares. On the Challenges of Fully Incremental Neural Dependency Parsing. In IJCNLP-AACL 2023, the 13th International Joint Conference on Natural Language Processing and the 3rd Conference of the Asia-Pacific Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics, Bali, Indonesia.

🎓 Education 2014-2015: Piano Professional Degree at Xan Viaño Professional Conservatory of Music. 2019-2023: Data Science and Engineering Degree at University of A Coruña. Honor distinction in the Final Degree Project: “Fully Incremental Parsing based on Neural Networks”. 2023-Present: Master in Artificial Intelligence at University of A Coruña. 💼 Experience 07/2021-09/2021 (full-time): Research Assistant at Centre for ICT Research. 09/2021-04/2022 (full-time): Department Collaboration Scholarship (University of A Coruña, MODES Group).
